rules.statement: A Proposal MUST only advance through the status transitions declared in .intent/META/enums.json. The permitted transition graph is: draft to pending or approved; pending to approved or rejected; approved to executing or rejected; executing to completed or failed. Any status assignment outside this graph is a lifecycle violation.

Every transition to the approved status MUST record a non-null approval_authority value from the closed set declared in .intent/META/enums.json under proposal_approval_authority. A transition to approved that omits or supplies an undeclared approval_authority value is constitutionally invalid.

The transition from approved to executing MUST record the identity of the claiming worker or CLI operator in the claimed_by field in the same atomic write that advances the status. A claim that does not supply a recognised claimer identity — a registered Worker UUID or the designated CLI sentinel UUID — MUST be rejected.

A claim attempt against a Proposal whose status is already executing, completed, or failed MUST be rejected without mutating state. Concurrent claims on the same Proposal MUST resolve to exactly one successful claimer; all subsequent attempts MUST receive a non-success result without side effects.
